title: Atlas-Based Image Segmentation
author: Okock Polycarp Omondi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The image segmentation algorithm extending Geodesic Active Contours method by an influence of prior knowledge of segmented shape is discussed in this work. It consists of two main steps. The first one is the training phase where a set of shape patterns is  created. Signed distance function is calculated to the boundaries of the shapes in atlas using an efficient distance calculation algorithm. The individual shapes represented by distance functions are registered to each other using standard gradient method. 

 Eigen shapes (atlas significant shapes) are calculated from the whole set of shapes using Principal Component Analysis. The second phase is image segmentation using Geodesic Active Contours method which is extended by an influence of the expected prior shape. Estimation of prior shape is original, based on the current segmentation and computed eigen shapes calculated from atlas of shape patterns.
